articles.title: Introducing NextPress: a modern editorial stack
articles.excerpt: Why we built a TanStack-first publishing experience and what problems it solves for content teams.
articles.author: Maya Chen
articles.authorBio: Runs the NextPress journal and cares about calm shipping cadences.

body.text: Why we built a TanStack-first publishing experience and what problems it solves for content teams. In this article we go deeper than the summary line—with examples you can reuse on your own team this week.
The core tension

Regardless of whether you sit in Company, most teams bump into the same friction: speed versus clarity. You want to ship, but you also want the work to hold up when someone reads it six months from now.
Prefer repeatable rituals over one-off heroics. Small loops compound.
We have seen the highest leverage come from narrowing scope, agreeing on a single source of truth, and publishing on a predictable rhythm—even when the artifact is imperfect.
What to try next

items: Pick one metric everyone can explain in a sentence.
Ship the smallest version that teaches you something.
Capture decisions in-line so future readers know why something looks the way it does.

body.text: If you take one habit from this piece, make it narrower scopes and clearer owners. Everything else gets easier once those two are true.
